results.description: The behavior tree represents a mobile robot tasked to visit a sequence of locations: ((0,0), (2,3), (4, 7), (5, 11)).  Each location is associated with a reading: (10, 20, 13, 27).  Location with a reading above 15 must be visited first. The available actions are: "MoveTo".
results.reference_xml:
<root BTCPP_format="4">
  <BehaviorTree ID="main_tree">
    <Sequence>
      <MoveTo x="5" y="11" threshold="27"/>
      <MoveTo x="2" y="3" threshold="20"/>
      <MoveTo x="4" y="7" threshold="13"/>
      <MoveTo x="0" y="0" threshold="10"/>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
results.validator_xml:
<root BTCPP_format="4" main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Fallback>
            <Sequence>
                <SubTree ID="TheTree" />
                <Done/>
            </Sequence>
            <Fail />
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="TheTree">
            <Sequence>
                <MoveTo x="1" y="1"/>
                <MoveTo x="3" y="3"/>
                <MoveTo x="0" y="0"/>
                <MoveTo x="2" y="2"/>
            </Sequence>
     </BehaviorTree>  
</root>
results.generated_raw:
High-reading locations first (>15), then rest.

- (2,3)=20, (5,11)=27 → reading >15, visit first
- (0,0)=10, (4,7)=13 → visit after

BT XML (BehaviorTree.CPP format):

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<root BTCPP_format="4" main_tree_to_execute="VisitByReading">
  <BehaviorTree ID="VisitByReading">
    <Sequence name="visit_priority_order">
      <!-- Reading > 15: visited first -->
      <MoveTo name="move_to_5_11" x="5" y="11" reading="27"/>
      <MoveTo name="move_to_2_3"  x="2" y="3"  reading="20"/>
      <!-- Reading <= 15: visited after -->
      <MoveTo name="move_to_4_7"  x="4" y="7"  reading="13"/>
      <MoveTo name="move_to_0_0"  x="0" y="0"  reading="10"/>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
```

Logic:
- `Sequence` = run children left→right, each must succeed before next.
- Order = sort by reading priority. High group (>15) before low group.
- 4 `MoveTo` actions, one per location.
results.generated_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<root BTCPP_format="4" main_tree_to_execute="VisitByReading">
  <BehaviorTree ID="VisitByReading">
    <Sequence name="visit_priority_order">
      <!-- Reading > 15: visited first -->
      <MoveTo name="move_to_5_11" x="5" y="11" reading="27"/>
      <MoveTo name="move_to_2_3"  x="2" y="3"  reading="20"/>
      <!-- Reading <= 15: visited after -->
      <MoveTo name="move_to_4_7"  x="4" y="7"  reading="13"/>
      <MoveTo name="move_to_0_0"  x="0" y="0"  reading="10"/>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
results.llm_judge_raw:
Correct

Tree visits locations ordered by reading threshold:
- Reading > 15 first: (5,11)→27, (2,3)→20
- Reading ≤ 15 after: (4,7)→13, (0,0)→10

Matches description requirement.

results.description: The behavior tree represents a mobile robot tasked to visit a sequence of locations: (0,0), (2,3), (4, 7), (5, 11).  During the navigation a location may become unreachable, if this happens, skip it and move to the next one.  The available actions are: "MoveTo", "isGoalReachable".
results.reference_xml:
<root BTCPP_format="4">
  <BehaviorTree ID="main_tree">
      <Sequence> 
          <ForceSuccess>
              <Parallel success_count="2" failure_count="1">                
                  <isGoalReachable/>                              
                  <MoveTo x="0" y="0"/> 
              </Parallel>
          </ForceSuccess>
          <ForceSuccess>
              <Parallel success_count="2" failure_count="1">                
                  <isGoalReachable/>                              
                  <MoveTo x="2" y="3"/> 
              </Parallel>
          </ForceSuccess>         
          <ForceSuccess>
              <Parallel success_count="2" failure_count="1">                
                  <isGoalReachable/>                              
                  <MoveTo x="4" y="7"/> 
              </Parallel>
          </ForceSuccess>   
          <ForceSuccess>
              <Parallel success_count="2" failure_count="1">                
                  <isGoalReachable/>                              
                  <MoveTo x="5" y="11"/> 
              </Parallel>
          </ForceSuccess>
      </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
results.validator_xml:
<root BTCPP_format="4" main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Fallback>
            <Sequence>
                <SubTree ID="TheTree" />
                <Done />
            </Sequence>
            <Fail />
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="TheTree">
        <Sequence>
            <Fallback>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <CheckReachable />
                    <MoveTo x="0" y="0" />
                </ReactiveSequence>
                <Continue />
            </Fallback>
            <Fallback>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <CheckReachable />
                    <MoveTo x="1" y="1" />
                </ReactiveSequence>
                <Continue />
            </Fallback>
            <Fallback>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <CheckReachable />
                    <MoveTo x="2" y="2" />
                </ReactiveSequence>
                <Continue />
            </Fallback>
            <Fallback>
                <ReactiveSequence>
                    <CheckReachable />
                    <MoveTo x="3" y="3" />
                </ReactiveSequence>
                <Continue />
            </Fallback>
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>
results.generated_raw:
Behavior tree below. Caveman explain after.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<root BTCPP_format="4" main_tree_to_execute="VisitLocations">

  <BehaviorTree ID="VisitLocations">
    <Sequence name="visit_all_in_order">

      <!-- Each location: try reach+move, but never block the sequence.
           ForceSuccess => failure (unreachable) is swallowed, move to next. -->

      <ForceSuccess>
        <Sequence name="goto_0_0">
          <Condition ID="isGoalReachable" x="0" y="0"/>
          <Action    ID="MoveTo"          x="0" y="0"/>
        </Sequence>
      </ForceSuccess>

      <ForceSuccess>
        <Sequence name="goto_2_3">
          <Condition ID="isGoalReachable" x="2" y="3"/>
          <Action    ID="MoveTo"          x="2" y="3"/>
        </Sequence>
      </ForceSuccess>

      <ForceSuccess>
        <Sequence name="goto_4_7">
          <Condition ID="isGoalReachable" x="4" y="7"/>
          <Action    ID="MoveTo"          x="4" y="7"/>
        </Sequence>
      </ForceSuccess>

      <ForceSuccess>
        <Sequence name="goto_5_11">
          <Condition ID="isGoalReachable" x="5" y="11"/>
          <Action    ID="MoveTo"          x="5" y="11"/>
        </Sequence>
      </ForceSuccess>

    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>

</root>
```

Logic:
- Top `Sequence` = visit in order.
- Per location inner `Sequence`: `isGoalReachable` first. Pass → `MoveTo`. Fail → inner sequence fails.
- `ForceSuccess` wrap = unreachable location returns SUCCESS to parent → skip, next location runs.
- `MoveTo` fail mid-move also swallowed → continues. Want hard-stop on move fail instead? Drop `ForceSuccess` reason — say so, I rewire.

Note: format = BehaviorTree.CPP v4. Different engine (py_trees, ROS BT)? Tell me, I convert.
results.generated_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<root BTCPP_format="4" main_tree_to_execute="VisitLocations">

  <BehaviorTree ID="VisitLocations">
    <Sequence name="visit_all_in_order">

      <!-- Each location: try reach+move, but never block the sequence.
           ForceSuccess => failure (unreachable) is swallowed, move to next. -->

      <ForceSuccess>
        <Sequence name="goto_0_0">
          <Condition ID="isGoalReachable" x="0" y="0"/>
          <Action    ID="MoveTo"          x="0" y="0"/>
        </Sequence>
      </ForceSuccess>

      <ForceSuccess>
        <Sequence name="goto_2_3">
          <Condition ID="isGoalReachable" x="2" y="3"/>
          <Action    ID="MoveTo"          x="2" y="3"/>
        </Sequence>
      </ForceSuccess>

      <ForceSuccess>
        <Sequence name="goto_4_7">
          <Condition ID="isGoalReachable" x="4" y="7"/>
          <Action    ID="MoveTo"          x="4" y="7"/>
        </Sequence>
      </ForceSuccess>

      <ForceSuccess>
        <Sequence name="goto_5_11">
          <Condition ID="isGoalReachable" x="5" y="11"/>
          <Action    ID="MoveTo"          x="5" y="11"/>
        </Sequence>
      </ForceSuccess>

    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>

</root>
results.llm_judge_raw:
Correct

The XML tree matches the description:

1. **Sequence of locations**: (0,0), (2,3), (4,7), (5,11) ✓
2. **Skip unreachable logic**: ForceSuccess wraps each location's check+move sequence. When `isGoalReachable` fails, the inner Sequence fails, ForceSuccess converts it to SUCCESS, and the outer Sequence continues to the next location ✓
3. **Actions used**: `isGoalReachable` (condition) + `MoveTo` (action) ✓
4. **Execution order**: Each location visited in sequence, failures don't block progression ✓

results.description: The behavior tree represents a mobile robot tasked asked to explore an area.  The available actions are: "GenerateNextDestination", "MoveTo", "isExplorationComplete".
results.validator_xml:
<root BTCPP_format="4" main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Fallback>
            <Sequence>
                <RetryUntilSuccessful num_attempts="10">
                    <SubTree ID="TheTree" />
                </RetryUntilSuccessful>
                <Done/>
            </Sequence>
            <Fail />
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="TheTree">
        <Sequence>
            <GenerateNextDestination/>
            <MoveToDestination/>
            <CheckForExplorationComplete/>
        </Sequence>
     </BehaviorTree>  
</root>
results.generated_raw:
Behavior tree = explore loop. Repeat {generate dest → move} until `isExplorationComplete`.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<root BTCPP_format="4">
  <BehaviorTree ID="ExploreArea">
    <Sequence>
      <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        <Fallback>
          <Inverter>
            <Condition ID="isExplorationComplete"/>
          </Inverter>
          <Sequence>
            <Action ID="GenerateNextDestination" destination="{next_dest}"/>
            <Action ID="MoveTo" goal="{next_dest}"/>
          </Sequence>
        </Fallback>
      </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
```

Logic:
- `isExplorationComplete` true → `Inverter` fails → `Fallback` tries explore branch... wait, that backwards. Fix reading:

Tick flow per loop:
- `Inverter(isExplorationComplete)`: not complete → returns SUCCESS → `Fallback` succeeds → loop continues.
- complete → `Inverter` returns FAILURE → explore `Sequence` runs `GenerateNextDestination`+`MoveTo`.

That inverted. Want: **not complete → explore; complete → stop**. Cleaner form:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<root BTCPP_format="4">
  <BehaviorTree ID="ExploreArea">
    <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
      <Fallback>
        <Inverter>
          <Condition ID="isExplorationComplete"/>
        </Inverter>
        <ForceFailure>
          <Sequence>
            <Action ID="GenerateNextDestination" destination="{next_dest}"/>
            <Action ID="MoveTo" goal="{next_dest}"/>
          </Sequence>
        </ForceFailure>
      </Fallback>
    </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
```

Flow:
- not complete → `Inverter`=SUCCESS → `Fallback`=SUCCESS → loop again (but explore branch skipped!). Bad.

Inverter+Fallback wrong tool. Simplest correct = **Sequence under loop**, condition gates first:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<root BTCPP_format="4">
  <BehaviorTree ID="ExploreArea">
    <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
      <Sequence>
        <Inverter>
          <Condition ID="isExplorationComplete"/>
        </Inverter>
        <Action ID="GenerateNextDestination" destination="{next_dest}"/>
        <Action ID="MoveTo" goal="{next_dest}"/>
      </Sequence>
    </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
```

Flow each tick:
- not complete → `Inverter`=SUCCESS → generate → move → `Sequence`=SUCCESS → `KeepRunningUntilFailure` loops.
- complete → `Inverter`=FAILURE → `Sequence`=FAILURE → loop stops → tree done.

Last one correct. Use it.
results.generated_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<root BTCPP_format="4">
  <BehaviorTree ID="ExploreArea">
    <Sequence>
      <KeepRunningUntilFailure>
        <Fallback>
          <Inverter>
            <Condition ID="isExplorationComplete"/>
          </Inverter>
          <Sequence>
            <Action ID="GenerateNextDestination" destination="{next_dest}"/>
            <Action ID="MoveTo" goal="{next_dest}"/>
          </Sequence>
        </Fallback>
      </KeepRunningUntilFailure>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
results.llm_judge_raw:
**Incorrect**

Tree logic fails to execute exploration loop:

1. **Inverter(isExplorationComplete)** succeeds while exploration NOT complete
   - Fallback returns SUCCESS on first child
   - Sequence (GenerateNextDestination, MoveTo) never executes
   - KeepRunningUntilFailure loops infinitely, doing nothing

2. Movement only happens when isExplorationComplete succeeds
   - Too late; exploration already done

Robot never actually generates destinations or moves during exploration. Structure is backwards.

Correct pattern would nest the Sequence INSIDE KeepRunningUntilFailure:
```xml
<KeepRunningUntilFailure>
  <Sequence>
    <Inverter><Condition ID="isExplorationComplete"/></Inverter>
    <Action ID="GenerateNextDestination"/>
    <Action ID="MoveTo"/>
  </Sequence>
</KeepRunningUntilFailure>
```

results.description: The behavior tree represents a manipulator tasked to find and reach a target.  The manipulator moves in different locations and check for the presence of a target object.  When the target is found, approach it.  The available actions are: "MoveTo", "CheckForTarget", "PickTarget".
results.validator_xml:
<root BTCPP_format="4" main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Fallback>
            <Sequence>
                <RetryUntilSuccessful num_attempts="10">
                    <SubTree ID="TheTree" />
                </RetryUntilSuccessful>
                <Done />
            </Sequence>
            <Fail />
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="TheTree">
        <Sequence>
            <Sequence>
                <MoveToNewConfiguration />
                <CheckForTarget />
            </Sequence>
            <ApproachTarget />
        </Sequence>
    </BehaviorTree>
</root>
results.generated_raw:
BT XML:

```xml
<root main_tree_to_execute="FindAndReachTarget">
  <BehaviorTree ID="FindAndReachTarget">
    <Sequence name="find_and_reach">
      <Fallback name="search_locations">
        <Sequence name="try_location_1">
          <MoveTo        location="loc_1"/>
          <CheckForTarget target="object"/>
        </Sequence>
        <Sequence name="try_location_2">
          <MoveTo        location="loc_2"/>
          <CheckForTarget target="object"/>
        </Sequence>
        <Sequence name="try_location_3">
          <MoveTo        location="loc_3"/>
          <CheckForTarget target="object"/>
        </Sequence>
      </Fallback>
      <PickTarget target="object"/>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
```

Logic:
- `Fallback` (search) = try locations til one `CheckForTarget` succeed. Each location: `MoveTo` then `CheckForTarget`.
- First location where target found → Fallback success → exit search.
- Then `PickTarget` approach/grab.
- Outer `Sequence`: search must succeed before pick run.

Note: if target found, `PickTarget` run. If no location have target, Fallback fail → whole tree fail.
results.generated_xml:
<root main_tree_to_execute="FindAndReachTarget">
  <BehaviorTree ID="FindAndReachTarget">
    <Sequence name="find_and_reach">
      <Fallback name="search_locations">
        <Sequence name="try_location_1">
          <MoveTo        location="loc_1"/>
          <CheckForTarget target="object"/>
        </Sequence>
        <Sequence name="try_location_2">
          <MoveTo        location="loc_2"/>
          <CheckForTarget target="object"/>
        </Sequence>
        <Sequence name="try_location_3">
          <MoveTo        location="loc_3"/>
          <CheckForTarget target="object"/>
        </Sequence>
      </Fallback>
      <PickTarget target="object"/>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
results.llm_judge_raw:
Looking at the description vs. XML structure:

**Description requires:**
1. Move to different locations ✓ (3 MoveTo actions in different location sequences)
2. Check for target presence at each ✓ (CheckForTarget in each sequence)
3. When target found, approach it ✓ (PickTarget executes after successful CheckForTarget)
4. Uses MoveTo, CheckForTarget, PickTarget ✓ (all three present)

**Tree logic:**
- Fallback tries 3 location sequences until one succeeds
- Each sequence: MoveTo → CheckForTarget (proceeds only if target found)
- When one succeeds, Fallback ends and PickTarget executes
- Pattern: search through locations → pick when found

**Correct**

results.description: The behavior tree represents a manipulator tasked to perform a pick and place routine.  The manipulator observe the item from multiple points of view and each time tries to estimate a grasp.  If the systems fails to identify a grasp after five observations, the action fails and the arm return to a resting position. When the grasp is successfully estimated, the manipulator moves to the "ready" state, picks the item and drop it to a predefined location, a chest in this case. After the drop, the arm moves to a resting position.  The available actions are:  "EstimateGrasp", "Pick", "Drop", "MoveManipulator".
results.reference_xml:
<root BTCPP_format="4">
  <BehaviorTree ID="main_tree">
      <RetryUntilSuccessful num_attempts="5">
          <Sequence>
              <EstimateGrasp/>
              <MoveManipulator state="ready"/>
              <Pick object="item"/>
              <Drop location="chest"/>
              <MoveManipulator state="resting"/>
          </Sequence>
      </RetryUntilSuccessful>
  </BehaviorTree>  
</root>
results.validator_xml:
<root BTCPP_format="4"  main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Fallback>
            <Sequence>
                <SubTree ID="TheTree" />
                <SubTree ID="TheTree" />
                <Done/>
            </Sequence>
            <Fail />
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>
    
    <BehaviorTree ID="TheTree">
        <Fallback>
            <Sequence>
                <RetryUntilSuccessful num_attempts="5">
                    <Sequence>
                        <PerformObservation />
                        <EstimateGrasp />
                    </Sequence>
                </RetryUntilSuccessful>
                <Pick />
                <MoveToDropPosition />
                <Drop />
                <MoveToRestPosition />
            </Sequence>
            <MoveToRestPosition />
        </Fallback>
     </BehaviorTree>  
</root>
results.generated_raw:
Pick-place BT. Caveman explain, then XML.

Logic:
- Retry grasp estimate up to 5 observations (move to viewpoint + EstimateGrasp each loop).
- Success → ready → Pick → Drop(chest) → rest.
- 5 fails → move rest + force fail whole action.

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<root BTCPP_format="4" main_tree_to_execute="PickAndPlace">

  <BehaviorTree ID="PickAndPlace">
    <Sequence name="pick_place_routine">

      <!-- Observe from up to 5 viewpoints, estimate grasp each time -->
      <Fallback name="grasp_or_abort">

        <RetryUntilSuccessful num_attempts="5" name="observe_loop">
          <Sequence name="observe_and_estimate">
            <Action ID="MoveManipulator" target="observation"/>
            <Action ID="EstimateGrasp"/>
          </Sequence>
        </RetryUntilSuccessful>

        <!-- All 5 observations failed: rest, then fail the action -->
        <ForceFailure name="abort_to_rest">
          <Action ID="MoveManipulator" target="rest"/>
        </ForceFailure>

      </Fallback>

      <!-- Grasp found: ready -> pick -> drop in chest -> rest -->
      <Action ID="MoveManipulator" target="ready"/>
      <Action ID="Pick"/>
      <Action ID="Drop" target="chest"/>
      <Action ID="MoveManipulator" target="rest"/>

    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>

</root>
```

Key nodes:
- `RetryUntilSuccessful num_attempts="5"` = 5 observation tries. Each tick re-runs move+estimate.
- `Fallback` = if retry exhausts, run abort branch.
- `ForceFailure` wrap rest move = arm rests **and** whole action reports failure (per spec).
- Success path runs after fallback returns success.

Note: BT.CPP retry re-ticks same child, so move-to-viewpoint must vary pose internally (next view) on each attempt. If you need explicit distinct poses, say so — swap retry for hardcoded sequence of 5 viewpoints.
results.generated_xml:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<root BTCPP_format="4" main_tree_to_execute="PickAndPlace">

  <BehaviorTree ID="PickAndPlace">
    <Sequence name="pick_place_routine">

      <!-- Observe from up to 5 viewpoints, estimate grasp each time -->
      <Fallback name="grasp_or_abort">

        <RetryUntilSuccessful num_attempts="5" name="observe_loop">
          <Sequence name="observe_and_estimate">
            <Action ID="MoveManipulator" target="observation"/>
            <Action ID="EstimateGrasp"/>
          </Sequence>
        </RetryUntilSuccessful>

        <!-- All 5 observations failed: rest, then fail the action -->
        <ForceFailure name="abort_to_rest">
          <Action ID="MoveManipulator" target="rest"/>
        </ForceFailure>

      </Fallback>

      <!-- Grasp found: ready -> pick -> drop in chest -> rest -->
      <Action ID="MoveManipulator" target="ready"/>
      <Action ID="Pick"/>
      <Action ID="Drop" target="chest"/>
      <Action ID="MoveManipulator" target="rest"/>

    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>

</root>

results.description: The behavior tree represents a manipulator tasked to push buttons in a specific sequence to transform raw materials into processed materials. Use "Button R" to receive the raw materials. Use "Button P" to start the processing. Use "Button C" to complete the processing.  The manipulator has to first receive the raw material, then start the processing, and finally complete the processing.  The manipulator has to periodically check the status of the materials to decide when the processing is complete. The available actions are: "PressButton", "EvaluateProcessing".
results.reference_xml:
<root BTCPP_format="4">
  <BehaviorTree ID="main_tree">
    <Parallel success_count="2" failure_count="1">                
      <EvaluateProcessing/>                              
      <Sequence>
        <PressButton button="Button R"/>
        <PressButton button="Button P"/>
        <PressButton button="Button C"/>
      </Sequence>
    </Parallel>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
results.validator_xml:
<root BTCPP_format="4" main_tree_to_execute="MainTree">
    <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
        <Fallback>
            <Sequence>
                <SubTree ID="TheTree" />
                <Done/>
            </Sequence>
            <Fail />
        </Fallback>
    </BehaviorTree>

    <BehaviorTree ID="TheTree">
        <SequenceWithMemory>
            <PressButton label="Button R"/>
            <PressButton label="Button P"/>
            <Sequence>
                <EvaluateProcessing/>
            </Sequence>
            <PressButton label="Button C"/>
        </SequenceWithMemory>
     </BehaviorTree>  
</root>
results.generated_raw:
Behavior tree XML (BehaviorTree.CPP style):

```xml
<root BTCPP_format="4">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <Sequence name="ProcessMaterials">
      <PressButton name="ReceiveRawMaterials" button="Button R"/>
      <PressButton name="StartProcessing" button="Button P"/>
      <RetryUntilSuccessful num_attempts="-1">
        <EvaluateProcessing name="CheckProcessingStatus"/>
      </RetryUntilSuccessful>
      <PressButton name="CompleteProcessing" button="Button C"/>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
```

Logic map:
- `Sequence` — enforce order: receive → start → check → complete.
- `PressButton button="Button R"` — receive raw.
- `PressButton button="Button P"` — start processing.
- `RetryUntilSuccessful` wrap `EvaluateProcessing` — periodic status check. Retry til processing done (returns SUCCESS). `num_attempts="-1"` = infinite poll.
- `PressButton button="Button C"` — complete.
results.generated_xml:
<root BTCPP_format="4">
  <BehaviorTree ID="MainTree">
    <Sequence name="ProcessMaterials">
      <PressButton name="ReceiveRawMaterials" button="Button R"/>
      <PressButton name="StartProcessing" button="Button P"/>
      <RetryUntilSuccessful num_attempts="-1">
        <EvaluateProcessing name="CheckProcessingStatus"/>
      </RetryUntilSuccessful>
      <PressButton name="CompleteProcessing" button="Button C"/>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>
results.llm_judge_raw:
Correct

The XML tree matches the description:

1. **Sequence enforces order**: R → P → status check → C ✓
2. **Button presses correct**: ReceiveRawMaterials (Button R), StartProcessing (Button P), CompleteProcessing (Button C) ✓
3. **Periodic status checking**: `RetryUntilSuccessful num_attempts="-1"` wraps `EvaluateProcessing`, meaning infinite retries until success—this represents periodic checking until processing completes ✓
4. **Actions match**: PressButton and EvaluateProcessing used as specified ✓

results.description: The behavior tree represents a mobile manipulator tasked to move between multiple stations to assemble a product.  The order of actions is the following.  Start from parking position and move to "Station A". In "Station A" pick up "Component A" and put it on the robot tray.  Move to "Station C" and deliver "Component A". Activate the assembly process. Collect the finished product and put it in the robot tray. Deliver the finished product to the parking position.  The available actions are: "moveTo", "Pick", "Drop", "ActivateProcess".
results.reference_xml:
<root BTCPP_format="4">
  <BehaviorTree ID="main_tree">
    <Sequence>
      <MoveTo location="Station A"/>
      <Pick from="Station A" item="Component A"/>
      <Drop to="tray"/>
      <MoveTo location="Station C"/>
      <Pick from="tray" item="Component A"/>
      <Drop to="Station C"/>
      <ActivateProcess/>
      <Pick from="Station C" item="Finished product"/>
      <Drop to="tray"/>
      <MoveTo location="Parking"/>
      <Pick from="tray" item="Finished product"/>
      <Drop to="Parking"/>
    </Sequence>
  </BehaviorTree>
</root>

```xml

      <moveTo          target="Station C"/>
```

Order map:
- moveTo A → Pick Component A (onto tray)
- moveTo C → Drop Component A (deliver)
- ActivateProcess (assembly)
- Pick finished product (onto tray)
- moveTo parking → Drop finished product

Root `Sequence`: each child must succeed before next. One fail = whole tree fail. Fits strict assembly order.
